description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:stable-git image for all jobs, runs with a docker:dind service, and defines CONTAINER_IMAGE, DOCKER_DRIVER and DOCKER_HOST variables. In the build stage add a build job that executes `docker login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" -p "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" "$CI_REGISTRY"` (if credentials are present), then `docker pull $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest || true`, `docker build --cache-from $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF .`, and finally `docker push $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF`. In the test stage add a test job that runs `env | sort`, logs in with the same docker login command, runs `docker run --rm "$CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF" env | sort` to show the container environment, creates a Docker network `rrdb` with `docker network create rrdb`, starts Selenium via `docker run --rm --network rrdb --name selenium --detach selenium/standalone-chrome:3.11.0-antimony`, pulls the built image `docker pull $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF`, waits for Selenium to be ready by repeatedly executing `docker run --rm --network rrdb --env SELENIUM_PORT=4444 "$CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF" curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail http://selenium:4444/wd/hub`, and finally runs the test suite with `docker run --rm --network rrdb --name app --env SELENIUM_PORT=4444 "$CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF" bundle exec rake`. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that logs in, pulls `docker pull $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF`, tags the image as latest with `docker tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest`, and pushes the latest tag using `docker push $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest`.
